turns.safety_veto_reason: Party A has stated participation incapacity: they report being cognitively impaired from sleep deprivation, unable to track requests, and not trusting themselves to make steady decisions about parenting matters. This is a CATEGORY 3 veto condition — explicit participation incapacity. Option work cannot proceed safely until this condition is resolved.

CATEGORY 3: Active safety signals — Party A has explicitly stated cognitive impairment affecting their capacity to track information and make decisions they would trust. The party has disclosed sleep deprivation, confusion, detail-mixing, and explicit distrust of their own judgment specifically on the parenting issue. This constitutes stated participation incapacity. The barrier is unresolved — Party A continues to express it in T4 even after Party B pressed to continue. Presenting substantive parenting options to a party in this cognitive state would create risk of uninformed or regretted agreement and would violate the process legitimacy concern Party A has articulated.
